page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

The server demonstrates strong memory and compute capabilities in benchmarks. It achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2181.0 and a strong multi-core score of 20357.0. Memory performance is highlighted by a read-only large block bandwidth of 192.31 GB/sec, though write bandwidth for large blocks is moderate at 77.38 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist in accelerating workloads like cryptography and vector calculations.

In application-specific benchmarks, the r8i-flex.16xlarge shows high efficiency in data processing and web serving. It delivers a top-tier static web serving throughput of 103.58 GB/sec and a strong Redis SET performance of 113.45 million operations per second. Database operations are also robust, with a Passmark database score of 28528.02. For machine learning workloads, the server processes 290.6 tokens per second for medium-sized 7B LLM prompts, though text generation for large 70B models drops to an average of 3.4 tokens per second.

This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that do not require local NVMe storage. The combination of high-speed DDR5 RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon cores makes it suitable for running large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It delivers 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz, and 512.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The server exhibits strong performance across benchmarks, notably in static web serving at 103.58 GB/sec, Redis SET operations at 113.45 million operations per second, and software compilation. Its processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enhancing cryptographic and vector processing. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale data processing, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and Intel Xeon processors for demanding enterprise database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Strong database performance with 113.45 million Redis SET operations per second
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Well-suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and web serving
